cards.back: A 2:1 multiplexer is a digital circuit that selects one of two input signals and passes it to the output, while ignoring the other input signal.
A 4:1 multiplexer is a digital circuit that selects one of four input signals and passes it to the output, while ignoring the other three input signals.
A 6:1 multiplexer is a digital circuit that selects one of six input signals and passes it to the output, while ignoring the other five input signals.
A 8:1 multiplexer is a digital circuit that selects one of eight input signals and passes it to the output, while ignoring the other seven input signals.
A 2:2 multiplexer is a digital circuit that selects one of two input signals and passes it to the output, while ignoring the other two input signals. It has a select line that controls the output.
